race.comment: PERFECT NIGHT (11) resumes having last been seen in the G2 Wakeful Stakes at Flemington. She should be sharp enough here, especially at 1300m having run a good race on debut over 1100m behind Point Barrow at Bendigo. She'll be very hard to hold out. SHOMA (6) resumes, now with the Busuttin & Young camp having last been seen running third in the G1 NZ 2000 Guineas at Riccarton. He's clearly a talented gelding who looks well placed from a soft gate here. WENTWORTH FALLS (9) resumes off a long break having shown good ability in his maiden campaign, close up in some strong maidens including a second placing to G1 winner Observer. This suits. MELIAN (5) just found one better on debut at Cranbourne but ran a strong race when in the market and can improve. SPARKLING LUCK (7) will be fitter off a good first-up run at Pakenham and has claims.
